---
title: "Many samples at once: the param_list"
description: "Process a whole cohort in one run, with per-sample and shared arguments."
order: 50
---

A **`param_list`** processes a whole cohort in a single run. It is a list where
**each entry is one sample**, carrying that sample's own arguments. Any argument
set **outside** the list applies to **every** sample.

```yaml
param_list:
  - id: sample_1
    input: data/sample_1.h5mu
    rna_min_counts: 2          # per-sample override
  - id: sample_2
    input: data/sample_2.h5mu
    rna_min_counts: 1          # per-sample override
rna_max_counts: 1000000        # shared: applied to all samples
```

Why it matters:

- **Global vs. per-sample is explicit** — shared defaults stay in one place;
  overrides sit next to the sample they affect.
- **One command, many samples** — no shell loops, no repeated invocations.
- **Parallelism** — Nextflow runs the entries concurrently.

See it end-to-end in the [Get started](../get-started/index.qmd) tutorial.
